next_step_text: Confidence 10% June 4th - Demo not successful, they have a strong materials management requirement we cannot support, they said they would come back to us, but looking unlikely it will progress, review over coming weeks and qualify out likely. 20th May - Positive disco call held (S/I/R summary below), next step arrange a live demo after Eid, agreed June 4th SITUATION Riyadh Cables operates on SAP

our_open_deliverables.text: Send the meeting slides and related documentation (including case study links or PDFs) to Hesham Taher after the 20 May discovery call.
Share the presentation slides and supporting materials from the 4 Jun demo with Hesham Taher and Housam Kayali.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Review the Zycus overview internally and follow up with Zycus to request a deep-dive session or confirm next steps for automation requirements.
Confirm and finalize demo dates (originally proposed 03 or 04 Jun) with VP of Supply Chain and other key stakeholders

competitive_position.summary: Primary current threat is Do-Nothing / Status Quo. Riyadh Cables previously evaluated and discontinued SAP Ariba (most recent) and Jaggaer; both failed implementations. Buyer is risk-averse and 'careful with selection regarding scope, budget, and expected savings' (20 May call). No active vendor competitor named on calls. GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba are listed in Salesforce Competitors__c but are dormant or historical (Ariba and Jaggaer are confirmed failed/eliminated; GEP and iValua not mentioned on calls). The real threat is that after the Zycus demo failed on the zero-touch PO requirement on 04 Jun, the buyer will revert to the manual SAP + Excel process rather than take another vendor risk. Rep confidence is 10% and qualify-out is likely. Zycus was positioned on 20 May as offering 'a better interface, easier deployment, and more intuitive use than competitors', but that advantage was lost when the demo exposed a product gap on a non-negotiable requirement. Ranking by current threat: (1) Do-Nothing (high, active, buyer silence for 27 days post-demo-failure signals this is the likely outcome); (2) SAP Ariba (low, dormant, previously failed); (3) JAGGAER (low, dormant, previously failed); (4) GEP Worldwide (low, dormant, not mentioned); (5) iValua (low, dormant, not mentioned).

competitors.name: Do-Nothing / Status Quo (remain with manual SAP + Excel process)
competitors.quote: No urgency for implementation; open to taking longer; demo failed on a hard requirement; buyer said they would come back but confidence is 10% and qualify-out likely.
competitors.source: Discovery call 20 May, Next Step 4 Jun
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of the manual process (hours per buyer per day, contract leakage, margin impact) and reframe the value case around the 80% of S2P that Zycus CAN automate, securing executive-level acknowledgment that the materials gap is acceptable; or partner with an integration specialist to deliver the hands-free automation.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Hesham evaluated Ariba and Jaggaer previously but discontinued; now exploring all market options carefully.
competitors.how_we_win: Ariba already eliminated (failed implementation). Not a current threat.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.quote: Hesham evaluated Ariba and Jaggaer previously but discontinued.
competitors.how_we_win: Jaggaer already eliminated (failed implementation). Not a current threat.

competitors.name: Do-Nothing / Status Quo
competitors.quote: Demo not successful, they have a strong materials management requirement we cannot support, they said they would come back to us, but looking unlikely it will progress, review over coming weeks and qualify out likely. Confidence 10%.
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe the zero-touch PO requirement: demonstrate that the two-click Zycus process delivers the same time savings (days to minutes) and eliminates the Excel manual matching pain that matters most, or commit zero-touch capability to product roadmap with a clear delivery date (e.g. Q4 2026). Secure executive sponsor (CFO or VP Supply Chain) to override the functional blocker and focus on strategic automation value vs. tactical click-count.

champion_strength.source: 20 May discovery call; 04 Jun demo call; Next_Step__c 04 Jun 2026 ('looking unlikely it will progress, confidence 10%'); zero contact for 27 days
champion_strength.summary: Champion is weak and at risk. Hesham Taher (GM Procurement) was the functional champion and sole active contact through 20 May discovery and 04 Jun demo. He articulated pain and requirements clearly and drove the evaluation, but after the demo failed on the zero-touch PO requirement he stated 'they would come back to us, but looking unlikely it will progress' and went silent for 27 days. Hesham does not have access to the Economic Buyer or executive sponsor (no CFO, CPO, CTO engagement), and the VP of Supply Chain (whom Hesham named as co-decision-maker) never joined the demo and has never been engaged. The deal is single-threaded through Hesham with no coach, no executive air cover, and no multi-threading. Champion strength is weak and cooling rapidly; the next move is to re-engage Hesham with a resolution to the product gap (roadmap commitment or reframe) and secure introductions to the VP of Supply Chain and Baha Eissa (CFO) to prevent the deal from being shelved.

sources: 20 May discovery call: 'Final decision path: Hesham and VP of Supply Chain to assess options then present to board for approval'
20 May call: Hesham stated 'Is there budget assigned at the moment or will the business case unlock the budget?' — implying no budget yet and Hesham must build the case

MEDDPICC 2.0 CRM: 'Not identified. Hesham Taher is the functional champion/influencer but has not been confirmed as the economic buyer. Housam Kayali (VP Supply Chain & Business Planning, iContract Group) joined the demo unexpectedly, suggesting a parent-group stakeholder with potential sign-off authority. No CFO, CPO, or senior executive sponsor has been engaged.'
Account contacts: Baha Eissa (Group CFO), Malik Assalameen (CTO) — neither engaged
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified. Hesham Taher (GM Procurement) is the functional buyer and project manager, but on the 20 May call he stated that 'Hesham and VP of Supply Chain will assess options then present to board for approval', meaning Hesham does not control the budget or final decision. Housam Kayali (VP Supply Chain & Business Planning, iContract Group) joined the 04 Jun demo unexpectedly, suggesting a parent-group stakeholder with potential sign-off authority, but his exact role in budget approval is unconfirmed and he has not been engaged since. No CFO, CPO, or senior executive sponsor has been mapped. The org chart on record includes Baha Eissa (Group CFO) and Malik Assalameen (CTO), but neither has been engaged. The deal is single-threaded through Hesham, who does not own the budget. With the demo failed and no executive sponsorship, the next move is to map the Economic Buyer (likely Baha Eissa or the VP of Supply Chain) and assess whether the product gap is a kill or can be reframed at the executive level.

recommended_moves: Assess with product/engineering whether zero-touch PO auto-creation can be committed to roadmap with Q4 2026 delivery date
Re-engage Hesham with product roadmap commitment or reframe (two-click process delivers same time-savings and eliminates Excel pain)
Secure introduction to VP of Supply Chain (named co-decision-maker, never engaged) and Baha Eissa (Group CFO, likely Economic Buyer)
